title: Cachrys cheilanthifolia
taxonomy: Cachrys cheilanthifolia is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is also known as a synonym of Seseli cheilanthifolium. It belongs to the genus Cachrys and is classified under the family Apiaceae. Cachrys cheilanthifolia is further classified into the order Apiales and the kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification is also supported by its morphological characteristics, which resemble those of other species in the same family and genus. The classification of Cachrys cheilanthifolia is based on the principles of taxonomy and is widely accepted by botanists and plant taxonomists.
uses: Cachrys cheilanthifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is said to have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. The plant's extracts may have antimicrobial and antifungal effects, which could help in fighting off infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. Some studies suggest that Cachrys cheilanthifolia may also have anticancer properties, but further investigation is required to establish its potential as a cancer treatment.

parts: Cachrys cheilanthifolia, also known as the Bitter Cachrys, is a plant species in the carrot family. The medicinal parts of Cachrys cheilanthifolia include its leaves and roots, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. side_effects: Cachrys cheilanthifolia, also known as Chervil or small-hoofed Hesperis, is a plant whose safety and side effects are not well-documented in mainstream medical literature. However, its close relative, Chervil (Anthriscus cerefolium), which is commonly used as a culinary herb, has been associated with certain side effects. These may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with blood-thinning medications. Further research is necessary to determine the potential side effects of Cachrys cheilanthifolia. intro: Cachrys cheilanthifolia, also known as Rock Celery or Wild Celery, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in reducing inflammation and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Cachrys cheilanthifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin conditions, and it is also used in traditional remedies for respiratory issues and digestive problems.
